description: A medicinal drink taken to restore health and vigor; what rebuilds strength. 'Wenu' names what revitalizes—the drink that returns energy and wellness.
sound_symbolism: The word renews strength. 'We' revives with returning energy, vitality stirring. Then 'nu' nourishes with grounded sustenance, strength building.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo wenu' (the tonic). Drunk for strength unlike 'moli' (balm) which is applied. For recovery, vitality, ongoing health.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Strengthening practice—regular practice as tonic for the mind, building resilience.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Tonics sustained health—herbal preparations drunk regularly to maintain vitality.
